note: EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2014/30/oj/eng resolves to official Directive 2014/30/EU (EMC Directive, OJ L 96, 29.3.2014). Standard EN IEC 61000-6-4:2019 exists and is harmonised under EMC Directive. GB 17799.4-2022 mandatory status confirmed via SAMR. Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/1326 reference marked UNVERIFIED in fragment — number plausible but not independently verified for this exact standard list entry. Gap claim (edition lag EN 2019 vs CN 2018-base) is directionally accurate. Mandatory status correct on both sides.

EUR-Lex URL for Directive 2014/30/EU confirmed. EN IEC 61000-6-2:2019 is harmonised under EMC Directive. CN equivalent GB/T 17799.2-2023 recommended standard consistent with SAMR practice. Structural gap (EU mandatory vs CN recommended for immunity) is accurate. Status fields correct.

SAMR URL https://openstd.samr.gov.cn/bzgk/std/newGbInfo?hcno=56520E6F3446EE95475015DC395F9EFB confirmed live and displays GB 17625.1-2022 (active from 2024-07-01). Standard number and mandatory status verified via SGS and SAMR sources. EN IEC 61000-3-2:2019/A1:2021 and EN IEC 61000-3-12:2011 are harmonised under Directive 2014/30/EU. Fragment note about GB/T 17625.7-2013 applicability tagged UNVERIFIED — correctly flagged. Gap claim directionally accurate.

EUR-Lex URL for EMC Directive confirmed. EN IEC 61000-3-3:2013/A2:2021 and EN IEC 61000-3-11:2019 exist and are harmonised under 2014/30/EU. CN equivalent GB 17625.2-2007 (based on IEC 61000-3-3:2005) — older edition gap correctly identified. Fragment self-tags as UNVERIFIED on CN status, which is appropriate. Gap claim directionally accurate (edition difference, missing CN equivalent for 16-75A band). Status fields correct.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2014/35/oj/eng confirmed for LVD 2014/35/EU. Voltage range (50-1000V AC / 75-1500V DC) verified from Commission LVD page. No notified body required for LVD self-declaration confirmed. CN CCC catalogue exclusion for grid-tied PV inverters marked UNVERIFIED in fragment — appropriate caution. Mandatory EU / voluntary CN status correct.

IEC webstore URL https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/6471 confirmed active and resolves to IEC 62109-2:2011 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters). EN IEC 62109-1:2010 and EN IEC 62109-2:2011 are harmonised under LVD 2014/35/EU. Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2023/2723 (OJ 13.12.2023) confirmed to exist as LVD harmonised standards decision — citation in fragment is accurate. Status field (both voluntary for the standard itself) is correct for harmonised standards which confer optional presumption of conformity.

EUR-Lex URL for LVD 2014/35/EU confirmed. Article 6 and Annex III obligations for technical documentation confirmed. Regulation (EC) 765/2008 and Decision 768/2008/EC correctly cited. 10-year retention period for technical documentation is correct per LVD Annex III. Mandatory EU / voluntary CN status correct.

EUR-Lex URL for Regulation (EU) 2016/631 (NC RfG, OJ L 112, 27.4.2016) confirmed. EN 50549-1:2019 / EN 50549-2:2019 exist as CENELEC harmonised standards under NC RfG — confirmed via iTeh catalog and standards search. UNVERIFIED caveat about uniform enforcement across all MS retained — appropriately flagged. GB/T 37408-2019 and GB/T 19964-2024 correctly described as recommended. Mandatory EU / voluntary CN status correct.

EUR-Lex URL for Regulation (EU) 2016/631 confirmed. EN 50549-1:2019 (LV, with A1:2023 amendment) and EN 50549-2:2019 (MV) confirmed as CENELEC standards supporting RfG. GB/T 19964-2024 superseding 2012 edition confirmed. NB/T 32004-2018 as mandatory industry standard confirmed. CN mandatory_status note about GB/T 19964 being effectively mandatory through grid interconnection agreements is accurate framing.

Source URL https://www.entsoe.eu/network_codes/rfg/ is the correct ENTSO-E RfG page (confirmed live). However the publisher field says 'ENTSO-E' which is correct but the fragment uses this as source for the EU regulatory requirement — the binding regulation is EUR-Lex 2016/631. URL itself resolves to a legitimate official source. Frequency range 47.5-51.5 Hz for EU is correct per EN 50549-1:2019 and Regulation 2016/631 Articles 13-14. CN disconnection below 48.0 Hz within 0.2s confirmed in GB/T 19964. Gap claim directionally accurate and widely cited. ENTSO-E IGD Frequency Ranges (2021) reference is plausible. URL is not dead, just secondary rather than primary regulatory source.

EUR-Lex URL for Regulation (EU) 2016/631 confirmed. EN 50549-1:2019 clause 4.5 (Voltage) cited — consistent with published standard structure. GB/T 19964-2024 LVRT at 0.2 pu for 625 ms, reactive current injection requirements confirmed. Gap claim about different ride-through profiles between EN 50549 and GB/T 19964 is directionally accurate and well-known in the industry. Mandatory on both sides confirmed.

Source URL https://standards.iteh.ai/catalog/standards/clc/948bd277-da91-40c7-b7e0-bbf561035638/en-50549-1-2019 confirmed — iTeh is a recognized CENELEC standards catalog distributor, page header confirms 'Source for standards'. EN 50549-1:2019 clause 4.9 anti-islanding 2-second detection limit is correct. EN 50549-10 (conformity assessment tests for anti-islanding) exists. Gap claim about different NDZ test methodology between EN 50549-10 and Chinese test methods is directionally accurate. Mandatory status correct on both sides.

EUR-Lex URL for Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS II, OJ L 174, 1.7.2011) confirmed. Directive 2015/863/EU adding four phthalates confirmed. EN IEC 63000:2018 harmonised standard for RoHS technical documentation confirmed. GB 26572-2025 effective 2027-08-01 adding phthalates aligns with known CN regulatory trajectory. GB/T 26572-2011 as current recommended standard and GB 26572-2025 as forthcoming mandatory confirmed. Status (mandatory EU / recommended CN currently) correct.

EUR-Lex URL for Directive 2012/19/EU (WEEE Directive, OJ L 197, 24.7.2012) confirmed. Article 17 authorised representative obligation confirmed. Category 4 (Large Equipment) classification for PV inverters consistent with WEEE scope. Crossed-out wheeled bin marking obligation (Annex IX) confirmed. Per-member-state registration requirement confirmed. Mandatory EU status correct.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2006/1907/oj/eng confirmed for REACH Regulation (EC) 1907/2006. Article 33 SVHC disclosure obligation confirmed. ECHA Candidate List with 240+ SVHCs and 45-day response requirement confirmed. 0.1% w/w threshold confirmed. MEE Order No. 12 (2020) CN equivalent correctly described as not imposing article-level SVHC disclosure. Mandatory EU / no CN equivalent status correct.

EUR-Lex URL for Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 (OJ L 191, 28.7.2023) confirmed. Row is correctly flagged as out of scope for standalone grid-tied PV inverters. Battery Regulation applicability to hybrid inverters with integrated storage is correctly noted. Out-of-scope framing is accurate.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2022/30/oj confirmed for Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/30. Mandatory application date 1 August 2025 verified via multiple official sources (SGS, TUV SUD, EUR-Lex). Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2025/138 (OJ 30.01.2025) listing EN 18031-1:2024 as harmonised standard confirmed. EN 18031-1:2024 scope (31 security requirements for internet-connected radio equipment) confirmed. Deferral Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2444 confirmed. NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555 citation confirmed. Note about C-PRAV reporting Commission repeal proposal is a prospective item (CRA transition) — the fragment describes the current applicable law accurately for the 2026 window.

EUR-Lex URL for Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/30 confirmed. EN 18031-2:2024 (40 requirements for personal data processing radio equipment) listed in Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2025/138 confirmed. GDPR Regulation (EU) 2016/679 existence and applicability confirmed. PIPL 2021 CN equivalent correctly described. Mandatory from 1 August 2025 (same as Art 3(3)(d)) confirmed. Status fields correct.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng confirmed for Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 (Cyber Resilience Act), OJ published 20.11.2024. Reporting obligations from 11 September 2026 and full product obligations from 11 December 2027 confirmed from official text. SBOM, vulnerability disclosure, and incident reporting to ENISA requirements confirmed. No CN pre-market product-lifecycle cybersecurity equivalent confirmed. Dates are accurate.

VDE FNN URL https://www.vde.com/en/fnn/topics/technical-connection-rules/power-generating-plants confirmed live and covers VDE-AR-N 4105. VDE-AR-N 4105 and VDE-AR-N 4110 are confirmed as mandatory requirements for grid connection in Germany. Einheitenzertifikat (unit certificate) requirement from DAkkS/VDE-accredited body confirmed. Fragment states VDE-AR-N 4105:2018-11 (updated 2026-03) — the update date claim is plausible but not independently verified; core standard exists and is mandatory. UNVERIFIED tag on specific accreditation body list appropriately retained. Gap claim (Chinese certifications not accepted) is accurate.

ENA URL https://www.energynetworks.org/electricity/engineering/distributed-generation/engineering-recommendation-g98.html confirmed. G98 Issue 2 and G99 Issue 2 both dated 10 March 2025 confirmed. UKCA requirement (not CE) for GB post-Brexit confirmed. ENA/NESO Type-Tested Equipment Register requirement confirmed. ROCOF thresholds marked UNVERIFIED in fragment — appropriate. Post-Brexit status clarification (GB not EU) correctly noted in mandatory_status. Mandatory within GB under UK law confirmed.

CEI URL https://www.ceinorme.it/en/free-documents-and-faqs/standards-cei-0-16-and-cei-0-21-tr-57-126-e-pas-57-127/ confirmed live and covers CEI 0-21 and CEI 0-16 standards. However: the fragment cites 'CEI 0-21:2019' and 'CEI 0-16:2019' as current editions, but CEI 0-21 has been superseded by CEI 0-21:2022-03 (confirmed from CEI norme search results showing versions up to 2022-03 and V4 variants). The standard still exists and is mandatory — the gap claim is still directionally accurate — but the edition year cited (2019) is outdated. The actual current edition is 2022. Content fields other than source.url are out of scope for URL fixing, but this is a substantive accuracy issue that needs human review.

ENTSO-E URL https://www.entsoe.eu/network_codes/rfg/ confirmed live and is the official RfG page from the European Network of TSOs. EN 50549-1:2019 and EN 50549-2:2019 confirmed as harmonised standards. Description of per-member-state NIM fragmentation is accurate. Chinese certification non-acceptance by EU DSOs confirmed. UNVERIFIED tag on exhaustive NIM document list appropriately retained. Mandatory status across all EU member states is correct.

URL https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/sectors/electrical-and-electronic-engineering-industries-eei/low-voltage-directive-lvd_en confirmed live as official EC DG GROW LVD page. Module A self-declaration (no Notified Body) for standard PV inverters under LVD and EMC confirmed. CCC catalogue exclusion for PV inverters noted correctly. Note on EN 62109-1/-2 should read EN IEC 62109-1/-2 — minor formatting inconsistency, not a factual error.

URL https://europa.eu/youreurope/business/product-requirements/compliance/technical-documentation-conformity/ is an official European Commission Your Europe portal page covering technical documentation obligations. LVD Article 15 / Annex IV, EMC Article 14 / Annex IV correctly cited. 10-year retention confirmed. Decision 768/2008/EC Module A and Regulation 765/2008 traceability correctly cited. Mandatory EU / no CN equivalent status correct.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32019R1020 confirmed for Regulation (EU) 2019/1020. Article 4 responsible person requirement (applicable from 16 July 2021) confirmed. Three qualifying categories (EU AR, importer, fulfilment service provider) confirmed. No CN equivalent confirmed. Mandatory EU status correct.

URL https://single-market-economy.ec.europa.eu/single-market/goods/ce-marking/manufacturers_en confirmed as official EC DG GROW CE marking page for manufacturers. 5mm minimum CE mark height confirmed. Manufacturer postal address, batch/serial number, importer address requirements confirmed. Regulation 765/2008 Article 30, Decision 768/2008 Annex II, and Regulation 2019/1020 Article 4(4)(c) correctly cited. Mandatory EU / domestic only CN status correct.

Row is tagged [UNVERIFIED] and [POLITICAL] in the fragment itself. Source URL https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/cybersecurity-policies is a real, live EC DG CONNECT page but it covers the EU Cybersecurity Act and NIS2 — it is NOT the source for the reported inverter funding restriction policy described in the row. No formal regulation text for the described inverter public-funding exclusion was published in the OJ as of the access date, as the fragment itself states. The source.url is therefore a mismatch: it references a cybersecurity policy overview page, not any document about the reported inverter exclusion from EU-funded projects. However, since no formal regulation exists yet, no correct official URL can be substituted — the row is correctly flagged UNVERIFIED. The reported policy context (EIB exclusion, April-May 2026 Commission guidance) aligns with reported news but cannot be confirmed against official sources. mandatory_status is correctly qualified as unverified/funding-eligibility only.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2014/53/oj/eng confirmed for RED 2014/53/EU (OJ L 153, 22.5.2014). RED applicability to radio-equipped PV inverters confirmed. SRRC type approval non-equivalence with RED confirmed. Conditional status for EU (applies only when radio module present) is correct and correctly coded. CN mandatory (domestic sale) status correct.

Source URL https://www.etsi.org/standards-search is the official ETSI standards search portal — valid secondary source for ETSI standards. ETSI EN 300 328 V2.2.2 (2019-07) confirmed as current published version for 2.4 GHz ISM band. ETSI EN 301 893 V2.1.1 for 5 GHz RLAN is the current harmonised version. The fragment UNVERIFIED note about whether V2.2.3 supersedes V2.2.2 is appropriate caution — no V2.2.3 found in search but ETSI work programme should be checked at publication. Conditional mandatory status (EU) correct. MIIT SRRC 2023 standard update (superseding 2002/2012 standards) correctly noted.

EUR-Lex URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32022D2191 confirmed for Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2022/2191 (RED harmonised standards, 8 November 2022). EN 301 489 restrictions since November 2022 confirmed — multiple standards published with restrictions in OJ. EN 62311:2008+A1:2017 for RF exposure confirmed. EN 301 489-1 and EN 301 489-17 correctly identified for broadband/5GHz data. The UNVERIFIED note about specific restriction clauses and 2025-2026 updates is appropriate — there have been subsequent amendments (2025/893 updates restrictions on EN 301 489-12 and EN 301 489-20). Conditional status correct.
